name: Duotone Kite Harnesses
meta_description: Duotone kitesurfing harnesses 2026 — Apex waist & seat harnesses with carbon shell. Expert fit advice. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Duotone Kite Harnesses 2026

Duotone's harness range is engineered for kitesurfers who demand precise fit and reliable load distribution. The Apex series features a carbon composite shell that wraps the lower back, distributing hook load across the entire waist panel rather than concentrating it at a single point. The result is less fatigue, better posture, and more comfortable sessions — especially during extended big-air or foil riding.

Waist vs Seat Harnesses

Waist harnesses like the Apex Curv sit on the lower ribs and offer maximum rotational freedom for wave riding, freestyle, and dynamic riding styles. Seat harnesses wrap around the hips and thighs, providing a locked-in feel that prevents ride-up — ideal for beginners and riders who prioritise comfort over agility.

Duotone's spreader bar system is compatible across their harness range, with standard and sliding hook options for height adjustment during riding.

Why Buy Duotone Harnesses at Surf Store

Harness fit is the most personal equipment choice in kitesurfing. Our team can advise on size, style, and spreader bar width for your body type and riding style. We stock Duotone harnesses from XS to XL.

FAQ

Q: Which Duotone harness for freestyle?
A: The Apex Curv waist harness — stiff carbon shell with maximum freedom for rotational tricks.

Q: How tight should a kite harness be?
A: Snug enough that it doesn't ride up when you're hooked in and loaded, but not so tight it restricts breathing.

Q: Can I use a Duotone harness for windsurfing?
A: Duotone kite harnesses use a different hook angle than windsurf harnesses. We recommend sport-specific models.

name: Duotone Wing Foils
meta_title: Duotone Wing Foils 2026 — Spirit & Glide Systems
meta_description: Duotone wing foil hydrofoils 2026 — Spirit front wings, Glide masts & fuselages. Build your ideal foil setup. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: duotone foil,duotone spirit wing,duotone glide mast
description_html:
Duotone Wing Foils 2026

Duotone's foil system for wing foiling is built around the modular Spirit front wing range and Glide mast platform. Every component — front wing, stabiliser, fuselage, and mast — can be swapped independently, letting you tune your foil for speed, lift, turning, or stability depending on conditions and skill level.

Front Wings

The Spirit front wing range spans from high-aspect speed wings (800cm²) to large freeride wings (2000cm²). Beginners should start with a larger wing (1500–2000cm²) for easy liftoff and forgiving flight. Advanced riders move to smaller, higher-aspect wings for speed and upwind performance.

Masts & Fuselages

Duotone Glide masts come in aluminium and carbon, from 60cm to 95cm. Start short (65–70cm) for safety while learning; progress to 85cm+ for cleaner riding above chop. Fuselage length affects turning — shorter fuselages turn tighter, longer ones track straighter.

Why Buy Duotone Foils at Surf Store

We build complete foil packages matched to your weight, discipline, and experience. Our team can advise on front wing area, mast length, and material choice for your budget.

FAQ

Q: Can I mix Duotone foil parts with other brands?
A: Duotone uses a brand-specific bolt pattern. Mixing within the Duotone system is recommended for compatibility.

Q: Aluminium or carbon mast for learning?
A: Aluminium — it's cheaper, durable, and crashes during learning are frequent. Upgrade to carbon once your technique is solid.

Q: What front wing for an 80kg beginner?
A: 1500–1800cm² provides easy liftoff and stable flight at low speeds.

name: Duotone Wing Boards
meta_title: Duotone Wing Foil Boards 2026 — Pace & Sky
meta_description: Duotone wing foil boards 2026 — Pace, Sky & Downwinder shapes. All volumes. Expert sizing advice. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: duotone wing board,duotone pace,duotone foil board
description_html:
Duotone Wing Foil Boards 2026

Duotone's wing foil board range covers the full progression from high-volume learning platforms to compact expert shapes. Every board features a reinforced foil track, recessed deck area, and optimised volume distribution that puts your weight over the foil for efficient pumping and takeoff.

The Range

The Sky Wing is the beginner board — wide, stable, with enough volume (100–130L) to support standing practice and first flights. The Pace series targets intermediate to advanced riders with reduced volume (60–90L) and refined rail shapes for carving and jumping. The Downwinder boards are elongated shapes designed for open-ocean downwind runs with a wing or prone foiling.

Choosing Your Board Volume

Start with your body weight in kg + 30–40L for comfortable learning. As technique improves, reduce volume gradually. An 80kg rider typically starts at 110–120L and progresses to 70–80L within one season.

FAQ

Q: Can I use a SUP board for wing foiling?
A: Some accept foil mounts, but dedicated wing boards with reinforced foil tracks perform far better and are safer.

Q: What board for an 80kg intermediate?
A: The Duotone Pace in 75–90L offers a good balance of stability and manoeuvrability.

Q: Hardboard or inflatable for wing foiling?
A: Hardboard for performance. Inflatable only if storage/transport is the primary concern.

name: Cabrinha Kite Bars
meta_description: Cabrinha Unify & 1X control systems 2026. Precision kite bars with integrated safety. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Cabrinha Kite Bars 2026

Cabrinha's control systems are designed as a unified platform — one bar system that works optimally across their entire kite range. The Unify bar is the flagship: clean line routing, a reliable push-away safety release, and an ergonomic grip shape that reduces hand fatigue during long sessions.

The Cabrinha Bar Range

The Unify is available in multiple line lengths (20m, 22m, 24m) with both 4-line and 5-line configurations. The 1X is the simplified version — a straightforward 4-line bar for riders who want reliability without the complexity of advanced trim options. Both bars feature Cabrinha's auto-swivel system that prevents line tangles during rotations.

Line Length Selection

Longer lines (24m) give more depower range and are preferred for light wind and big-air riding. Shorter lines (20m) provide faster kite response and are favoured by wave riders who need quick redirects above the surf.

FAQ

Q: Does the Cabrinha Unify work with older Cabrinha kites?
A: Yes — Cabrinha maintains cross-season bar compatibility. The Unify works with 2024+ kites without modification.

Q: Can I use a Cabrinha bar with a Duotone kite?
A: Not recommended. Bar geometry, safety systems, and line attachment differ between brands.

Q: What bar width for an adult rider?
A: The 52cm Unify suits most adult riders. Smaller riders may prefer the 46cm option.

name: Cabrinha Kiteboards
meta_description: Cabrinha kiteboards 2026 — Spectrum, Ace, XCaliber & surf shapes. All sizes. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Cabrinha Kiteboards 2026

Cabrinha's kiteboard range reflects the brand's Hawaiian surf heritage — boards that feel alive in the water, with responsive flex patterns and shapes that track confidently through chop. From entry-level twin-tips to premium surf shapes, every Cabrinha board is designed for riders who want connection to the water rather than just riding on top of it.

Twin-Tips

The Spectrum is Cabrinha's all-around twin-tip — moderate rocker, comfortable flex, and reliable pop for freeriding and progression. The Ace adds stiffness and a refined outline for aggressive freeriding and freestyle. The XCaliber is the performance freestyle board with a flat centre section for maximum pop and precision landings.

Surf & Foil Boards

Cabrinha's directional surf boards bring surfboard DNA to strapless kite riding — short, responsive, and designed for bottom turns in real waves. Their foil board range offers low-volume shapes optimised for kite foiling with centred stance positioning.

FAQ

Q: What size Cabrinha board for an 80kg freerider?
A: The Spectrum in 138–142cm is ideal for all-round freeride at 80kg.

Q: Are Cabrinha boards sold with pads and straps?
A: Most Cabrinha boards include pads and straps. Check individual product listings for details.

Q: Can I use Cabrinha boards with any kite brand?
A: Yes — kiteboards are universal and work with kites from any brand.

name: NeilPryde Wetsuits
meta_description: NeilPryde wetsuits 2026 — Wizard, Mission & Nexus series. GBS construction for warm, flexible performance. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
NeilPryde Wetsuits 2026

NeilPryde brings decades of materials science expertise to their wetsuit range. Originally known for sail construction, NeilPryde applies the same attention to panel layout, seam placement, and material selection to their neoprene products. The result is wetsuits that flex where you need movement and insulate where you need warmth.

The NeilPryde Wetsuit Range

The Wizard is the premium line — featuring GBS (glued and blind-stitched) seams, liquid-seal wrist and ankle cuffs, and a 3D pre-shaped knee that eliminates restriction in footstraps. The Mission series offers mid-range performance with flatlock seams and quality neoprene at a lower price. Thicknesses range from 3/2mm for spring/autumn to 5/4mm for winter sailing.

Why NeilPryde Wetsuits

NeilPryde wetsuits are designed by and for water sports riders — not general surfers. The cut accommodates the wider arm movements of windsurfing and the crouched stance of kitesurfing, with pre-bent arms and knees that reduce fatigue during active riding.

FAQ

Q: How do NeilPryde wetsuits compare to ION?
A: Both are excellent. NeilPryde focuses on windsurf-specific fit; ION offers a wider range of thicknesses and entry styles. Our team can help you choose.

Q: What thickness for Mediterranean windsurfing?
A: 3/2mm covers most of the season. Add a 5/4mm for winter months (December–March).

Q: Do NeilPryde wetsuits run true to size?
A: NeilPryde uses European sizing. Consult their height/weight chart — our team can verify your fit.

name: NeilPryde Windsurf Masts
meta_title: NeilPryde Windsurf Masts 2026 — SDM & RDM Carbon
meta_description: NeilPryde windsurf masts 2026 — X1, X3 & X6 carbon series. SDM & RDM. Expert compatibility advice. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: neilpryde mast,neilpryde sdm mast,neilpryde rdm mast
description_html:
NeilPryde Windsurf Masts 2026

NeilPryde produces some of the most precisely manufactured windsurf masts in the sport. Their mast range is defined by carbon percentage tiers: the X1 (100% carbon) for racing, the X3 (75% carbon) for performance freeride, and the X6 (60% carbon) for recreational use. Each tier maintains NeilPryde's exacting bend curve specifications.

SDM vs RDM

SDM masts are standard for freeride and race sails above 5.5m. RDM masts suit wave sails and smaller sails where the narrower diameter allows the mast to flex through the mast pocket during impacts.

Matching Masts to Sails

Every NeilPryde sail publishes a recommended mast IMCS rating and luff length. Using a NeilPryde mast with a NeilPryde sail ensures the designed bend curve is achieved — delivering optimal sail profile and performance.

FAQ

Q: Can I use a NeilPryde mast with Duotone sails?
A: Yes, if IMCS ratings and luff lengths match. But brand-matched combinations deliver the best profile accuracy.

Q: How much difference does carbon percentage make?
A: Significant. 100% carbon is 30–40% lighter than 60% carbon and delivers faster power response. The difference is most noticeable in strong wind.

Q: Are NeilPryde masts 2-piece?
A: Yes — all NeilPryde windsurf masts are 2-piece (top and bottom) for transport convenience.

name: NeilPryde Wings
meta_description: NeilPryde wing foil wings 2026 — inflatable wings for all levels. Expert sizing advice. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: neilpryde wing,neilpryde wing foil,neilpryde inflatable wing
description_html:
NeilPryde Wings 2026

NeilPryde applies their extensive sail design expertise to the wing foiling category. NeilPryde wings use proprietary canopy materials derived from windsurf sail technology — delivering a stiff, responsive feel that translates wind power efficiently into forward drive on the foil.

The NeilPryde Wing Range

NeilPryde wings are available from 3.5m to 6.5m, covering high-wind speed sessions through to light-wind cruising. The construction features reinforced leading edges, rigid handles, and transparent windows for downwind visibility during transitions. Each wing is supplied with a wrist leash and compact carry bag.

Why NeilPryde Wings

NeilPryde's background in sail aerodynamics gives their wings a performance edge in upwind angle and power stability. Riders transitioning from windsurfing to wing foiling often find NeilPryde wings familiar — the power delivery feels like a well-trimmed sail rather than a draggy canopy.

FAQ

Q: What size NeilPryde wing for an 80kg rider?
A: 5.0–5.5m covers the widest range of conditions (12–25 knots).

Q: Are NeilPryde wings compatible with any foil?
A: Yes — wings are universal. Any wing works with any foil and board combination.

Q: How durable are NeilPryde wings?
A: NeilPryde's sail-grade materials are among the most durable in the wing market. Expect 2–3 seasons with proper care.

name: ION Kite & Windsurf Harnesses
meta_description: ION harnesses 2026 — Riot Curv, Apex, Element series. Kite & windsurf. Expert fit advice. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: ion harness,ion riot curv,ion apex harness
description_html:
ION Harnesses 2026

ION produces harnesses for both kitesurfing and windsurfing — each designed with sport-specific hook angles, load paths, and back panel shapes. ION's harness range spans from entry-level models with comfortable padding to pro-level carbon-shell designs that distribute load precisely across the lower back and hips.

Kite Harnesses

The Riot Curv is ION's flagship kite harness — a pre-shaped carbon composite back panel with memory foam padding that moulds to your body over time. The Apex is the mid-range option with excellent support and a lower price. The Element is the entry-level waist harness for beginners who want quality construction without premium features.

Windsurf Harnesses

ION windsurf harnesses use a different hook angle and wider spreader bar than kite models. Available in waist and seat designs, they distribute the sail's pull across the body for comfortable freeride and wave sessions.

FAQ

Q: Can I use the same harness for kiting and windsurfing?
A: Not recommended — hook angles and load paths differ. Use a sport-specific harness for safety and comfort.

Q: What size ION harness?
A: ION uses European sizing (S, M, L, XL) based on waist measurement. Measure at the navel level.

Q: How long does a harness last?
A: 3–5 seasons with proper care. Replace if the back panel shows cracks or the hook mechanism wears.

name: ION Neoprene Boots
meta_description: ION neoprene boots 2026 — 2mm to 6mm. Round & split toe. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: ion boots,ion neoprene boots,ion surf shoes
description_html:
ION Neoprene Boots 2026

Cold feet end sessions faster than anything else. ION neoprene boots protect against cold water, sharp reef, rocks, and sea urchins while maintaining enough flexibility for comfortable board riding. The range spans from thin 1.5mm reef walkers for tropical protection to thick 6mm winter boots for sub-10°C water.

Round Toe vs Split Toe

Round-toe boots are warmer and more comfortable for walking. Split-toe boots separate the big toe, providing better grip and feel in footstraps — preferred by windsurfers and kitesurfers who need precise strap control.

Thickness Guide

  • 1.5–2mm: Reef protection in warm water (22°C+)
  • 3mm: Spring/autumn use (15–20°C)
  • 5–6mm: Winter use (below 12°C)

FAQ

Q: Should boots fit tight or loose?
A: Snug — they stretch slightly when wet. A loose boot causes blisters and lets cold water in.

Q: Can I wear boots inside footstraps?
A: Yes — adjust straps slightly wider. Split-toe boots give better strap feel.

Q: How do I dry boots quickly?
A: Turn inside out after each use. Never use a heater or dryer — heat damages neoprene.

name: Gaastra Windsurf Sails
meta_description: Gaastra windsurf sails 2026 — Cosmic, Matrix & Boost series. Race tech at great value. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: gaastra sails,gaastra cosmic sail,gaastra matrix sail
description_html:
Gaastra Windsurf Sails 2026

Gaastra sails combine Dutch engineering heritage with competitive pricing — delivering race-influenced performance for club and recreational windsurfers who want reliable, well-made sails without paying premium brand prices. Under the Boards & More umbrella, Gaastra benefits from shared R&D with Duotone while maintaining its own distinct sail designs.

The Gaastra Sail Range

The Cosmic is the freerace sail — stiff, powerful, and designed for maximum speed on flat water. It uses a cambered profile that locks in under load, providing consistent power across a wide wind range. The Matrix is the all-around freeride sail — easy to rig, forgiving in gusts, and suitable for everything from light-wind cruising to moderate wave sailing. The Boost is the entry-level sail — a rotational design that's simple to rig and offers a smooth, progressive power curve for learners.

Why Gaastra Sails

Gaastra sails offer typically 20–30% lower pricing than equivalent Duotone or NeilPryde models while sharing production quality standards. For club racers and recreational sailors who want performance without the premium, Gaastra is a smart choice.

FAQ

Q: Are Gaastra sails compatible with Duotone masts?
A: Yes — match IMCS ratings and luff lengths. Gaastra sails work well with Duotone masts of the correct specification.

Q: What Gaastra sail for a beginner?
A: The Boost in 5.5–6.5m. It's forgiving, easy to rig, and provides smooth power for learning to plane.

Q: Does Surf Store stock all Gaastra sail sizes?
A: We carry the full size range from 4.0m to 9.5m. Contact us for specific size availability.

name: Nobile Kiteboards
meta_description: Nobile kiteboards 2026 — NHP, 50Fifty, T5 & Split models. Polish craftsmanship. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Nobile Kiteboards 2026

Nobile kiteboards are manufactured in Puck, Poland with a focus on wood-core construction, precise flex patterns, and durable build quality. The brand's boards have earned a reputation in the freestyle community for consistent pop and reliable performance — at prices that undercut the premium European brands.

The Nobile Board Range

The NHP (Nobile High Performance) is the flagship freestyle board — stiff, poppy, and precision-shaped for trick landings. The NHP Carbon adds full carbon layup for reduced weight and even faster response. The 50Fifty Evolution is the all-around freeride board — balanced flex, comfortable pads, and reliable upwind tracking. The T5 is the entry-level option with forgiving flex and wide outline for learners.

Nobile also produces Split boards — twin-tips that separate into two halves for compact travel, with a secure locking mechanism that holds under load.

Why Buy Nobile at Surf Store

We stock Nobile kiteboards with the full IFS binding system. Our team can advise on sizing, flex pattern, and binding setup for your weight and riding style.

FAQ

Q: What size Nobile board for an 80kg freerider?
A: NHP 138–142cm or 50Fifty 140cm. Size down 2–4cm for dedicated freestyle.

Q: Are Nobile split boards as stiff as one-piece?
A: Slightly less stiff at the join point, but modern Nobile split boards perform very close to one-piece equivalents.

Q: Do Nobile boards come with pads and straps?
A: Most models include the Nobile IFS binding system. Check individual product specs.

name: Nobile Foils
meta_description: Nobile hydrofoils 2026 — kite foil sets, front wings & masts. Modular system. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Nobile Hydrofoils 2026

Nobile's foil system offers a modular platform for kite foiling and wing foiling. Built with the same quality standards as their kiteboards, Nobile foils use aluminium and carbon components with precise machining tolerances. The system is designed for riders stepping into foiling — with stable, forgiving front wings and robust mast construction that handles learning-phase crashes.

Foil Components

Nobile's front wings range from freeride-oriented designs (1200–1600cm²) to higher-aspect speed wings. Masts come in aluminium for durability during learning and carbon for experienced riders seeking lighter weight. The fuselage connects front and rear wings with adjustable shim angles for flight tuning.

Complete Sets vs Components

For beginners, we recommend a complete Nobile foil set — matched components tested together. Experienced riders can mix front wings and masts within the Nobile system to tune performance.

FAQ

Q: Are Nobile foils compatible with their kiteboards?
A: Nobile foil boards use a standard Tuttle box or dedicated foil track. Check board specifications for compatibility.

Q: What front wing for learning to kite foil?
A: 1400–1600cm² provides stable, forgiving flight for beginners at moderate speeds.

Q: Can I use Nobile foils for wing foiling?
A: Yes — the same foil system works for both kite and wing foiling with the appropriate board.

name: Tabou Windsurf Boards
meta_title: Tabou Windsurf Boards 2026 — Rocket, 3S & Da Bomb
meta_description: Tabou windsurfing boards 2026 — freeride, wave & freestyle. French-engineered. Free EU shipping.
description_html:
Tabou Windsurf Boards 2026

Tabou's board range is shaped in France and manufactured to deliver smooth, predictable performance for recreational and club-level windsurfers. The brand focuses on accessible shapes that make good riders better — boards that plane early, track confidently, and respond intuitively to weight shifts and footwork.

The Tabou Board Range

The Rocket is the freeride flagship — wide tail, moderate rocker, and a planing surface optimised for early takeoff. Available from 115L to 165L, it suits riders from 60kg to 100kg+. The 3S (Sun, Surf, Slalom) bridges freeride and wave — compact enough for small waves, fast enough for flat-water blasting. The Da Bomb is the pure wave board for riders who sail in consistent surf. The Manta targets freestyle with a flat rocker for pop and spin-friendly rails.

Tabou + Gaastra Packages

Tabou boards pair naturally with Gaastra sails — both brands share the Boards & More distribution. We build complete Tabou + Gaastra windsurf setups at competitive bundle pricing.

FAQ

Q: Which Tabou board for a beginner?
A: The Rocket in 145L–165L. Wide, stable, and planes early in moderate wind.

Q: How does Tabou compare to Fanatic?
A: Fanatic targets advanced riders with premium construction. Tabou offers accessible performance at a lower price — excellent value for club sailors.

Q: Does Surf Store carry all Tabou models?
A: We stock the Rocket, 3S, and Da Bomb. Contact us for specific size availability.

name: Fanatic SUP Boards
meta_description: Fanatic SUP boards 2026 — Ray Air, Fly Air, Diamond Air & race boards. iSUP & hardboard. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: fanatic sup board,fanatic ray air,fanatic fly air isup
description_html:
Fanatic SUP Boards 2026

Fanatic produces one of the most comprehensive SUP board ranges in Europe — from compact city boards to open-ocean touring shapes, in both inflatable and composite hardboard construction. Every board benefits from Fanatic's 40+ years of board-building expertise, with shapes refined through testing by their international rider team.

Inflatable SUP (iSUP)

The Ray Air is the touring flagship — narrow nose, efficient tracking, and multi-layer drop-stitch construction that achieves hardboard-like stiffness. The Fly Air is the all-round family board — 10'4" wide with a stable platform for beginners and casual paddlers. The Diamond Air is designed for women and lighter riders with adjusted volume and deck pad sizing.

Hardboard SUP

Fanatic hardboard SUPs use vacuum sandwich construction with either bamboo or carbon skins. The Bee is a compact city board for short paddles. The Falcon is the race board — narrow, fast, with a displacement hull for competitive speed.

FAQ

Q: iSUP or hardboard for a beginner?
A: iSUP — easier to transport, store, and more forgiving during falls. Performance is excellent on modern drop-stitch boards.

Q: What board width for an 80kg paddler?
A: 32–34" for all-round stability. Go narrower (30") for speed; wider (34–36") for yoga or fishing.

Q: Do Fanatic iSUPs come with paddles?
A: Some packages include paddles. Check individual product listings. We sell Fanatic paddles separately.

name: Fanatic Windsurf Boards
meta_title: Fanatic Windsurf Boards 2026 — Blast, Falcon & Grip
meta_description: Fanatic windsurfing boards 2026 — freeride, race, wave & freestyle. Award-winning shapes. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: fanatic windsurf board,fanatic blast,fanatic falcon slalom
description_html:
Fanatic Windsurf Boards 2026

Fanatic's windsurfing board range is shaped by a team that includes multiple PWA World Champions. From the entry-level Gecko to the podium-winning Falcon slalom board, every shape is tested in competitive conditions and refined for the production models that reach riders worldwide.

The Range

The Blast is the freeride best-seller — wide, stable, and quick to plane. Available in volumes from 100L to 160L. The Falcon is the slalom board — narrow and fast, designed for flat-water speed. The Grip and Quad are the wave boards — compact shapes with responsive rails for critical turns. The Gecko is the beginner board with an optional centreboard for light-wind learning. The Stingray is the freewave crossover — wave-capable but equally fun on flat water.

Construction Levels

Standard construction uses polyethylene skin for durability. HRS adds wood sandwich for better performance-to-weight. TE (Team Edition) uses full carbon for minimum weight. X is Fanatic's premium line with hand-finished carbon layup.

FAQ

Q: What Fanatic board for a beginner?
A: Gecko 148L–156L for absolute beginners. Blast 135L–155L once you can plane.

Q: Are Fanatic boards compatible with all fin types?
A: Fanatic uses US Box, Tuttle, and slot-box depending on model. Check specs for your board.

Q: Wave board — Grip or Quad?
A: Grip (tri-fin) for powerful, drawn-out turns. Quad for looser, skatey wave riding. Both are excellent.

name: Point-7 Windsurf Sails
meta_title: Point-7 Windsurf Sails 2026 — AC-K, Spy, Salt & MC
meta_description: Point-7 sails 2026 — race, freeride, wave & freestyle. Racing DNA at fair prices. Free EU shipping.
meta_keywords: point-7 sails,point7 ac-k sail,point7 spy sail
description_html:
Point-7 Windsurf Sails 2026

Point-7 sails are designed by Andrea Cucchi — a professional racer who applies personal competition experience to every sail in the range. The result is a focused lineup where every model has genuine racing DNA, at prices that are typically 20–30% below the premium brands. For riders who prioritise on-water performance over brand prestige, Point-7 offers exceptional value.

The Point-7 Sail Range

The AC-K is the racing flagship — a fully cambered slalom sail tested at PWA-level speeds. Sizes from 5.8m to 9.5m. The AC-F is the foil-optimised version with reduced luff length and controlled power for windsurf foiling. The Spy is the all-round freeride sail — easy to rig, wide wind range, and forgiving power delivery. The Salt is the wave sail — compact, lightweight, and built for manoeuvrability. The MC is the freestyle sail with a stiff, poppy feel for aerial tricks.

Why Point-7

Independent speed tests consistently show Point-7 AC-K performing within 1–2% of sails costing 30% more. For club racers and performance-oriented freeride riders, this is compelling value.

FAQ

Q: What mast for Point-7 sails?
A: Point-7 publishes IMCS and luff recommendations per model. Compatible with most brand masts at the correct specification.

Q: Is the AC-K suitable for non-racers?
A: Experienced freeride riders who want maximum speed will enjoy the AC-K. For casual sailing, the Spy is more appropriate.

Q: Does Surf Store stock all Point-7 sizes?
A: We carry the full size range. Contact us for specific size and model availability.
